office_does: State representatives write and vote on state laws and budgets, oversee state government, and represent their legislative district.
race_blurb: The open-seat primary includes housing-authority chair Chase Foster, anti-establishment candidate Liz Hallock, and Yakima Councilmember Reedy Berg.

candidates.summary: Foster chairs the Kennewick Housing Authority and emphasizes housing supply, public safety, education, and limits on taxes and spending.
highlights: Calls for limits on taxes and state spending [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly calls for limits on taxes.

spending.basis: He explicitly calls for limits on state spending.

housing.basis: Housing supply is his stated mechanism for affordability.

experience.basis: Housing-authority governance supplies an institutional record, while he has no legislative tenure.

candidates.summary: Hallock is a repeat candidate running an anti-establishment campaign focused on accountability and concentrated political power.

reform.basis: Limiting concentrated political power is the central stated purpose of her anti-establishment campaign.

experience.basis: She presents as an outsider and the dossier establishes no prior elected office.

candidates.summary: Berg is a Yakima city councilmember who emphasizes infrastructure, affordability, public safety, and economic opportunity.
highlights: Serves on the Yakima City Council [S1]
Emphasizes infrastructure, affordability, and economic opportunity [S1]

experience.basis: Current city-council service supplies a public governing record.
